title: S-Rest iterative-state restoration contract
description: Specifies that a non-closing loop, peeling pass, refinement, or recursive transition returns to the tactic input signature with H_0, E, I, R, V, Q, A, ledgers, budgets, and live handles restored. The record operationalizes the S-Rest loop-restoration lemma schema in the structural exhaustion framework (branch_closure_methodology_extended.tex, "Lemma schemas").

schema.description: Required contract discriminator; it must be the literal `S-Rest`.

fromStateRef.description: Required nonempty stable identifier of the state at the beginning of the iterative pass.

toStateRef.description: Required nonempty stable identifier of the state produced by the non-closing pass and proposed as the next iteration's input.

restoredSignature.description: Required mathematical statement that the destination state inhabits the tactic's declared input-state type, with every coordinate needed for another pass restored.

informal.description: Required nonempty prose statement of the full restored signature and its relation to the source signature.

latex.description: Optional LaTeX formulation of the restoration invariant or state-transition typing judgment.

formalLanguage.description: Optional name and version of the formal language used to encode restoration.

formal.description: Optional machine-checkable proof statement that the destination satisfies the required signature.

restoredItems.description: Required category-by-category inventory of restored state; all five categories must be present even when a list is empty.

baselineHypotheses.description: Required list of theorem/counterexample assumptions available again after the pass, with any changed carrier object stated explicitly.

items.description: One restored baseline hypothesis and the evidence that it holds for the destination state.

admissibilityConditions.description: Required list of domain conditions making another pass legal, such as branch-state well-typedness, residual membership, interface compatibility, consumer-trigger readiness, ledger-domain validity, vocabulary availability, or certificate-handle validity.

items.description: One restored admissibility predicate, including the object to which it applies.

invariants.description: Required list of structural and quantitative facts restored for the next iteration, including minimality consequences, forbidden-response statements, density or rank bounds, monotonicity facts, capacity estimates, exact-type equalities, ledger invariants, budget signs, and trigger facts.

items.description: One restored invariant and its stable identifier.

ledgers.description: Required list of accounting records carried or recomputed for the next pass, with demand set D, payer set P, assignment pi, weights w, capacities kappa, lower-bound target l, budget currency, slack entries, domains, and update rules sufficient to prevent loss or double counting of resources.

items.description: One restored ledger identifier together with its post-pass balance/invariant.

payloadHandles.description: Required list of still-valid routed objects or certificate references that the next pass may consume; stale handles must be omitted or replaced by newly proved ones.

items.description: One live payload or certificate handle and its declared producer/type.

evidence.description: Required nonempty list of sources proving the complete restoration claim and every listed item.

items.description: One evidence record supporting state restoration.

kind.description: Required evidence category from the fixed evidence-kind alphabet.

reference.description: Required nonempty locator for the loop invariant, S-Rest record, state-update lemma, S-Pers proof, ledger check, slack-table row, payload-route check, or formal transition.

hash.description: Optional integrity hash for an implementation or formal artifact that performs or checks restoration.

notes.description: Optional note mapping the source to specific restored categories or explaining a changed state component.
